section.p: The Retrieval and AI Layer is responsible for transforming user queries into grounded, context-aware responses using a multi-stage retrieval pipeline.
This system goes beyond basic vector similarity search by incorporating:
These steps significantly improve both retrieval precision and context quality, enabling more accurate and explainable responses.

section.title: 3.4.1 Retrieval Pipeline
section.p: The runtime pipeline executes the following stages:

li.p: Hybrid Scoring (vector + keyword + hierarchy + metadata + intent)

section.title: 3.4.2 Query Expansion
section.p: User queries are expanded using rule-based techniques to improve recall.
This step helps bridge vocabulary gaps between:

section.title: 3.4.3 Query Intent Classification
section.p: The system performs lightweight, rule-based query intent classification prior to retrieval scoring.
Intent categories:

li.p: identity — questions about the author (e.g., "Who is Michael Taylor?")
experience — questions about career history, roles, and projects
technical — questions about system design, architecture, or implementation

section.p: Intent is determined using keyword matching against the user query.
This classification influences retrieval behavior by adjusting metadata-based scoring weights.
This approach enables:

li.p: prioritization of profile-related documents for identity queries
improved alignment between query intent and retrieved content
better separation between technical and biographical responses

section.p: The classifier is intentionally lightweight and deterministic to avoid additional model overhead.

section.title: 3.4.4 Query Embedding
section.p: The expanded query is converted into a vector embedding using:

section.title: 3.4.5 Vector Retrieval (FAISS)
section.p: A FAISS index is used to perform approximate nearest neighbor search.
This stage prioritizes recall, returning a broad candidate pool for downstream filtering.

section.title: 3.4.6 Hybrid Scoring
section.p: Each candidate chunk is rescored using a hybrid approach combining:
Metadata extracted from Markdown frontmatter is actively used during scoring to improve relevance.
Intent-aware boosting dynamically adjusts scoring weights based on the classified query intent, improving alignment between user intent and retrieved content.
This improves performance in cases where:

section.title: 3.4.7 Diversity Selection (MMR)
section.p: Maximal Marginal Relevance (MMR) is applied to reduce redundancy.
MMR balances:
This prevents over-representation of a single document or section.

section.title: 3.4.8 Semantic Reranking
section.p: A cross-encoder model is used for final ranking:
This model evaluates query–chunk pairs directly and produces a more precise relevance ranking than embedding similarity alone.

section.title: 3.4.9 Document-Aware Aggregation
section.p: Unlike naive RAG systems, this implementation performs document-level grouping.
Steps:

li.p: Score each document using its highest-ranked chunk

section.p: This ensures:

li.p: stronger alignment with how humans consume documentation

section.title: 3.4.10 Knowledge Graph Expansion
section.p: After document selection, the system expands context using a document-level knowledge graph.
The graph is stored as an adjacency map:
Expansion behavior:

li.p: retrieve related documents for selected top documents
include additional supporting context when relevant

section.p: This enables:

section.title: 3.4.11 Context Assembly
section.p: The final context is constructed from:
The context is structured to:

section.title: 3.4.12 Response Generation
section.p: The final prompt is sent to the language model:
The model generates a response grounded entirely in retrieved context.
This ensures:

li.p: factual consistency with the knowledge base

section.title: 3.4.13 Metadata Usage
section.p: Metadata extracted from Markdown frontmatter is actively used during retrieval scoring.
Metadata fields include:
Metadata contributes to retrieval through:

li.p: direct matching between query terms and metadata values
weighted scoring bonuses for matching categories and tags
integration into hybrid scoring alongside vector and keyword signals

section.p: This enables:

li.p: better alignment between user intent and content type
enhanced discoverability of portfolio and profile content

section.p: Metadata-aware scoring is further enhanced by query intent classification, allowing the system to prioritize different content types dynamically.
